   118ŌåÆ                            contentDescription = "Back"
   119ŌåÆ                        )
   120ŌåÆ                    }
   121ŌåÆ                },
   122ŌåÆ                actions = {
   123ŌåÆ                    if (uiState.actor != null) {
   124ŌåÆ                        IconButton(onClick = {
   125ŌåÆ                            val profileHandle = uiState.actor!!.handle
   126ŌåÆ                            val normalizedHandle = if (profileHandle.startsWith("@")) profileHandle else "@$profileHandle"
   127ŌåÆ                            val profileUrl = "https://hackers.pub/$normalizedHandle"
   128ŌåÆ                            val sendIntent = Intent().apply {
   129ŌåÆ                                action = Intent.ACTION_SEND
   130ŌåÆ                                putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_TEXT, profileUrl)
   131ŌåÆ                                type = "text/plain"
   132ŌåÆ                            }
   133ŌåÆ                            context.startActivity(Intent.createChooser(sendIntent, null))
   134ŌåÆ                        }) {
   135ŌåÆ                            Icon(
   136ŌåÆ                                imageVector = Icons.Outlined.Share,
   137ŌåÆ                                contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.share)
   138ŌåÆ                            )
   139ŌåÆ                        }
   140ŌåÆ                    }
   141ŌåÆ                    if (!uiState.isViewer && uiState.actor != null) {
   142ŌåÆ                        ProfileActionMenu(
   143ŌåÆ                            followsViewer = uiState.followsViewer,
   144ŌåÆ                            viewerBlocks = uiState.viewerBlocks,
   145ŌåÆ                            isPerformingAction = uiState.isPerformingAction,
   146ŌåÆ                            onRemoveFollower = { viewModel.removeFollower() },
   147ŌåÆ                            onBlock = { viewModel.blockActor() },
   148ŌåÆ                            onUnblock = { viewModel.unblockActor() }
   149ŌåÆ                        )
   150ŌåÆ                    }
   151ŌåÆ                }
   152ŌåÆ            )
   153ŌåÆ        }
   154ŌåÆ    ) { paddingValues ->
   155ŌåÆ        Box(
   156ŌåÆ            modifier = Modifier
   157ŌåÆ                .fillMaxSize()
   158ŌåÆ                .padding(paddingValues)
   159ŌåÆ        ) {
   160ŌåÆ            when {
   161ŌåÆ                uiState.isLoading && uiState.actor == null -> {
   162ŌåÆ                    FullScreenLoading()
   163ŌåÆ                }
   164ŌåÆ                uiState.error != null && uiState.actor == null -> {
   165ŌåÆ                    ErrorMessage(
   166ŌåÆ                        message = uiState.error ?: stringResource(R.string.error_generic),
   167ŌåÆ                        onRetry = { viewModel.loadProfile(handle) }
   168ŌåÆ                    )
   169ŌåÆ                }
   170ŌåÆ                uiState.actor != null -> {
   171ŌåÆ                    PullToRefreshBox(
   172ŌåÆ                        isRefreshing = uiState.isRefreshing,
   173ŌåÆ                        onRefresh = { viewModel.refresh() }
   174ŌåÆ                    ) {
   175ŌåÆ                        LazyColumn(state = listState) {
   176ŌåÆ                            item {
   177ŌåÆ                                ProfileHeader(
   178ŌåÆ                                    avatarUrl = uiState.actor!!.avatarUrl,
   179ŌåÆ                                    name = uiState.actor!!.name,
   180ŌåÆ                                    handle = uiState.actor!!.handle,
   181ŌåÆ                                    bio = uiState.bio,
   182ŌåÆ                                    isViewer = uiState.isViewer,
   183ŌåÆ                                    viewerFollows = uiState.viewerFollows,
   184ŌåÆ                                    followsViewer = uiState.followsViewer,
   185ŌåÆ                                    viewerBlocks = uiState.viewerBlocks,
   186ŌåÆ                                    isPerformingAction = uiState.isPerformingAction,
   187ŌåÆ                                    onFollowClick = { viewModel.followActor() },
   188ŌåÆ                                    onUnfollowClick = { viewModel.unfollowActor() },
   189ŌåÆ                                    onMentionClick = onProfileClick
   190ŌåÆ                                )
   191ŌåÆ                                HorizontalDivider()
   192ŌåÆ                            }
   193ŌåÆ
   194ŌåÆ                            items(
   195ŌåÆ                                items = uiState.posts,
   196ŌåÆ                                key = { it.id }
   197ŌåÆ                            ) { post ->
   198ŌåÆ                                PostCard(
   199ŌåÆ                                    post = post,
   200ŌåÆ                                    onClick = { onPostClick(post.sharedPost?.id ?: post.id) },
   201ŌåÆ                                    onProfileClick = onProfileClick,
   202ŌåÆ                                    onReplyClick = { onReplyClick(post.sharedPost?.id ?: post.id) },
   203ŌåÆ                                    onShareClick = {
   204ŌåÆ                                        val targetId = post.sharedPost?.id ?: post.id
   205ŌåÆ                                        if (post.viewerHasShared) {
   206ŌåÆ                                            viewModel.unsharePost(targetId)
   207ŌåÆ                                        } else {
   208ŌåÆ                                            viewModel.sharePost(targetId)
   209ŌåÆ                                        }
   210ŌåÆ                                    },
   211ŌåÆ                                    onQuoteClick = { onQuoteClick(post.sharedPost?.id ?: post.id) },
   212ŌåÆ                                    onExternalShareClick = {
   213ŌåÆ                                        val displayPost = post.sharedPost ?: post
   214ŌåÆ                                        val shareUrl = displayPost.url ?: displayPost.iri
   215ŌåÆ                                        if (shareUrl != null) {
   216ŌåÆ                                            val sendIntent = Intent().apply {
   217ŌåÆ                                                action = Intent.ACTION_SEND
   218ŌåÆ                                                putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_TEXT, shareUrl)
   219ŌåÆ                                                type = "text/plain"
   220ŌåÆ                                            }
   221ŌåÆ                                            context.startActivity(Intent.createChooser(sendIntent, null))
   222ŌåÆ                                        }
   223ŌåÆ                                    },
   224ŌåÆ                                    onQuotedPostClick = onPostClick
   225ŌåÆ                                )
   226ŌåÆ                                HorizontalDivider(thickness = 0.5.dp)
   227ŌåÆ                            }
   228ŌåÆ
   229ŌåÆ                            if (uiState.isLoadingMore) {
   230ŌåÆ                                item {
   231ŌåÆ                                    LoadingItem()
   232ŌåÆ                                }
   233ŌåÆ                            }
   234ŌåÆ                        }
   235ŌåÆ                    }
   236ŌåÆ                }
   237ŌåÆ            }
   238ŌåÆ        }
   239ŌåÆ    }
   240ŌåÆ}

   283ŌåÆ                onClick = {

   287ŌåÆ                enabled = !isPerformingAction
   288ŌåÆ            )
   289ŌåÆ        }
   290ŌåÆ    }

   336ŌåÆ            Text(

   338ŌåÆ                style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   339ŌåÆ                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   340ŌåÆ                textAlign = TextAlign.Center
   341ŌåÆ            )
   342ŌåÆ
   343ŌåÆ            if (!isViewer && (followsViewer || viewerBlocks)) {
   344ŌåÆ                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(8.dp))
   345ŌåÆ                RelationshipTags(
   346ŌåÆ                    followsViewer = followsViewer,
   347ŌåÆ                    viewerBlocks = viewerBlocks
   348ŌåÆ                )
   349ŌåÆ            }
   350ŌåÆ        }
   351ŌåÆ
   352ŌåÆ        if (!isViewer) {
   353ŌåÆ            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(12.dp))
   354ŌåÆ
   355ŌåÆ            if (viewerFollows) {

   359ŌåÆ                    colors = ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(
   360ŌåÆ                        containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error
   361ŌåÆ                    ),
   362ŌåÆ                    modifier = Modifier.width(200.dp)
   363ŌåÆ                ) {
   364ŌåÆ                    if (isPerformingAction) {
   365ŌåÆ                        CircularProgressIndicator(
   366ŌåÆ                            modifier = Modifier.size(16.dp),
   367ŌåÆ                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onError,
   368ŌåÆ                            strokeWidth = 2.dp
   369ŌåÆ                        )
   370ŌåÆ                    } else {
   371ŌåÆ                        Text(stringResource(R.string.unfollow))
   372ŌåÆ                    }
   373ŌåÆ                }
   374ŌåÆ            } else {
   375ŌåÆ                Button(
   376ŌåÆ                    onClick = onFollowClick,
   377ŌåÆ                    enabled = !isPerformingAction,
   378ŌåÆ                    modifier = Modifier.width(200.dp)
   379ŌåÆ                ) {
   380ŌåÆ                    if (isPerformingAction) {
   381ŌåÆ                        CircularProgressIndicator(
   382ŌåÆ                            modifier = Modifier.size(16.dp),
   383ŌåÆ                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onPrimary,
   384ŌåÆ                            strokeWidth = 2.dp
   385ŌåÆ                        )
   386ŌåÆ                    } else {
   387ŌåÆ                        Text(stringResource(R.string.follow))
   388ŌåÆ                    }
   389ŌåÆ                }
   390ŌåÆ            }
   391ŌåÆ        }
   392ŌåÆ
   393ŌåÆ        if (!bio.isNullOrBlank()) {
   394ŌåÆ            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(12.dp))
   395ŌåÆ            HtmlContent(
   396ŌåÆ                html = bio,
   397ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   398ŌåÆ                onMentionClick = onMentionClick
   399ŌåÆ            )
   400ŌåÆ        }
   401ŌåÆ    }
   402ŌåÆ}
   403ŌåÆ
   404ŌåÆ@Composable
   405ŌåÆprivate fun RelationshipTags(
   406ŌåÆ    followsViewer: Boolean,
   407ŌåÆ    viewerBlocks: Boolean

   409ŌåÆ    Row(horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(8.dp)) {
   410ŌåÆ        if (followsViewer) {

   413ŌåÆ                style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   414ŌåÆ                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary,
   415ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier
   416ŌåÆ                    .background(
   417ŌåÆ                        MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary.copy(alpha = 0.15f),
   418ŌåÆ                        RoundedCornerShape(50)
   419ŌåÆ                    )
   420ŌåÆ                    .padding(horizontal = 8.dp, vertical = 4.dp)
   421ŌåÆ            )
   422ŌåÆ        }
   423ŌåÆ
   424ŌåÆ        if (viewerBlocks) {
   425ŌåÆ            Text(
   426ŌåÆ                text = stringResource(R.string.blocked),
   427ŌåÆ                style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   428ŌåÆ                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error,
   429ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier
   430ŌåÆ                    .background(
   431ŌåÆ                        MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error.copy(alpha = 0.15f),
   432ŌåÆ                        RoundedCornerShape(50)
   433ŌåÆ                    )
   434ŌåÆ                    .padding(horizontal = 8.dp, vertical = 4.dp)
   435ŌåÆ            )
   436ŌåÆ        }
   437ŌåÆ    }
   438ŌåÆ}

    15ŌåÆimport dagger.hilt.android.AndroidEntryPoint

    18ŌåÆ
    19ŌåÆdata class DeepLinkData(
    20ŌåÆ    val token: String,
    21ŌåÆ    val code: String
    22ŌåÆ)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆ@AndroidEntryPoint
    25ŌåÆclass MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {
    26ŌåÆ
    27ŌåÆ    private var deepLinkData by mutableStateOf<DeepLinkData?>(null)
    28ŌåÆ
    29ŌåÆ    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
    30ŌåÆ        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
    31ŌåÆ        handleDeepLink(intent)
    32ŌåÆ        enableEdgeToEdge()
    33ŌåÆ        setContent {
    34ŌåÆ            HackersPubTheme {
    35ŌåÆ                Surface(
    36ŌåÆ                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
    37ŌåÆ                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.background
    38ŌåÆ                ) {
    39ŌåÆ                    HackersPubApp(deepLinkData = deepLinkData)
    40ŌåÆ                }
    41ŌåÆ            }
    42ŌåÆ        }
    43ŌåÆ    }
    44ŌåÆ
    45ŌåÆ    override fun onNewIntent(intent: Intent) {
    46ŌåÆ        super.onNewIntent(intent)
    47ŌåÆ        handleDeepLink(intent)
    48ŌåÆ    }
    49ŌåÆ
    50ŌåÆ    private fun handleDeepLink(intent: Intent?) {
    51ŌåÆ        val data = intent?.data ?: return
    52ŌåÆ        if (data.scheme == "hackerspub" && data.host == "verify") {
    53ŌåÆ            val token = data.getQueryParameter("token")
    54ŌåÆ            val code = data.getQueryParameter("code")
    55ŌåÆ            if (token != null && code != null) {
    56ŌåÆ                deepLinkData = DeepLinkData(token = token, code = code)
    57ŌåÆ            }
    58ŌåÆ        }
    59ŌåÆ    }
    60ŌåÆ}

    96ŌåÆsealed class DetailScreen(val route: String) {
    97ŌåÆ    data object SignIn : DetailScreen("signin?token={token}&code={code}") {
    98ŌåÆ        fun createRoute(token: String? = null, code: String? = null): String {
    99ŌåÆ            val params = mutableListOf<String>()
   100ŌåÆ            if (token != null) params.add("token=$token")
   101ŌåÆ            if (code != null) params.add("code=$code")
   102ŌåÆ            return if (params.isEmpty()) "signin" else "signin?${params.joinToString("&")}"
   103ŌåÆ        }
   104ŌåÆ    }
   105ŌåÆ    data object Compose : DetailScreen("compose?replyTo={replyTo}&quoteOf={quoteOf}") {
   106ŌåÆ        fun createRoute(replyTo: String? = null, quoteOf: String? = null): String {
   107ŌåÆ            val params = mutableListOf<String>()
   108ŌåÆ            if (replyTo != null) params.add("replyTo=$replyTo")
   109ŌåÆ            if (quoteOf != null) params.add("quoteOf=$quoteOf")
   110ŌåÆ            return if (params.isEmpty()) "compose" else "compose?${params.joinToString("&")}"
   111ŌåÆ        }
   112ŌåÆ    }
   113ŌåÆ    data object PostDetail : DetailScreen("post/{postId}") {
   114ŌåÆ        fun createRoute(postId: String) = "post/$postId"
   115ŌåÆ    }
   116ŌåÆ    data object Profile : DetailScreen("profile/{handle}") {
   117ŌåÆ        fun createRoute(handle: String) = "profile/$handle"
   118ŌåÆ    }
   119ŌåÆ}
   120ŌåÆ
   121ŌåÆ@Composable
   122ŌåÆfun HackersPubApp(
   123ŌåÆ    deepLinkData: pub.hackers.android.DeepLinkData? = null,
   124ŌåÆ    viewModel: AppViewModel = hiltViewModel()
   125ŌåÆ) {
   126ŌåÆ    val navController = rememberNavController()
   127ŌåÆ    val isLoggedIn by viewModel.isLoggedIn.collectAsState(initial = false)
   128ŌåÆ
   129ŌåÆ    val fontSizePercent by viewModel.preferencesManager.fontSizePercent.collectAsState(initial = 100)
   130ŌåÆ
   131ŌåÆ    // Handle deep link for verification
   132ŌåÆ    LaunchedEffect(deepLinkData) {
   133ŌåÆ        deepLinkData?.let {
   134ŌåÆ            navController.navigate("signin?token=${it.token}&code=${it.code}")
   135ŌåÆ        }
   136ŌåÆ    }
   137ŌåÆ
   138ŌåÆ    ProvideInAppBrowserUriHandler(preferencesManager = viewModel.preferencesManager) {
   139ŌåÆ    CompositionLocalProvider(LocalFontScale provides (fontSizePercent / 100f)) {
   140ŌåÆ
   141ŌåÆ    val bottomNavItems = if (isLoggedIn) {
   142ŌåÆ        listOf(Screen.Timeline, Screen.Notifications, Screen.Explore, Screen.Search, Screen.Settings)
   143ŌåÆ    } else {
   144ŌåÆ        listOf(Screen.Explore, Screen.Search, Screen.Settings)
   145ŌåÆ    }
   146ŌåÆ
   147ŌåÆ    val navBackStackEntry by navController.currentBackStackEntryAsState()
   148ŌåÆ    val currentDestination = navBackStackEntry?.destination
   149ŌåÆ    val showBottomBar = bottomNavItems.any { it.route == currentDestination?.route }
   150ŌåÆ
   151ŌåÆ    Scaffold(
   152ŌåÆ        bottomBar = {
   153ŌåÆ            if (showBottomBar) {
   154ŌåÆ                NavigationBar(
   155ŌåÆ                    modifier = Modifier
   156ŌåÆ                        .windowInsetsPadding(WindowInsets.navigationBars)
   157ŌåÆ                        .height(56.dp),
   158ŌåÆ                    windowInsets = WindowInsets(0)
   159ŌåÆ                ) {
   160ŌåÆ                    bottomNavItems.forEach { screen ->
   161ŌåÆ                        val selected = currentDestination?.hierarchy?.any { it.route == screen.route } == true
   162ŌåÆ                        NavigationBarItem(
   163ŌåÆ                            icon = {
   164ŌåÆ                                Icon(
   165ŌåÆ                                    if (selected) screen.selectedIcon else screen.unselectedIcon,
   166ŌåÆ                                    contentDescription = stringResource(screen.titleResId)
   167ŌåÆ                                )
   168ŌåÆ                            },
   169ŌåÆ                            label = null,
   170ŌåÆ                            selected = selected,
   171ŌåÆ                            onClick = {
   172ŌåÆ                                navController.navigate(screen.route) {
   173ŌåÆ                                    popUpTo(navController.graph.findStartDestination().id) {
   174ŌåÆ                                        saveState = true
   175ŌåÆ                                    }
   176ŌåÆ                                    launchSingleTop = true
   177ŌåÆ                                    restoreState = true
   178ŌåÆ                                }
   179ŌåÆ                            }
   180ŌåÆ                        )
   181ŌåÆ                    }
   182ŌåÆ                }
   183ŌåÆ            }
   184ŌåÆ        }
   185ŌåÆ    ) { innerPadding ->
   186ŌåÆ        NavHost(
   187ŌåÆ            navController = navController,
   188ŌåÆ            startDestination = if (isLoggedIn) Screen.Timeline.route else Screen.Explore.route,
   189ŌåÆ            modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding)
   190ŌåÆ        ) {
   191ŌåÆ            composable(Screen.Timeline.route) {
   192ŌåÆ                TimelineScreen(
   193ŌåÆ                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   194ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   195ŌåÆ                    },
   196ŌåÆ                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   197ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   198ŌåÆ                    },
   199ŌåÆ                    onComposeClick = { replyTo ->
   200ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(replyTo))
   201ŌåÆ                    },
   202ŌåÆ                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   203ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   204ŌåÆ                    }
   205ŌåÆ                )
   206ŌåÆ            }
   207ŌåÆ
   208ŌåÆ            composable(Screen.Notifications.route) {
   209ŌåÆ                NotificationsScreen(
   210ŌåÆ                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   211ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   212ŌåÆ                    },
   213ŌåÆ                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   214ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   215ŌåÆ                    },
   216ŌåÆ                    onComposeClick = {
   217ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute())
   218ŌåÆ                    }
   219ŌåÆ                )
   220ŌåÆ            }
   221ŌåÆ
   222ŌåÆ            composable(Screen.Explore.route) {
   223ŌåÆ                ExploreScreen(
   224ŌåÆ                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   225ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   226ŌåÆ                    },
   227ŌåÆ                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   228ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   229ŌåÆ                    },
   230ŌåÆ                    onReplyClick = { postId ->
   231ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(postId))
   232ŌåÆ                    },
   233ŌåÆ                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   234ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   235ŌåÆ                    },
   236ŌåÆ                    onComposeClick = {
   237ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute())
   238ŌåÆ                    },
   239ŌåÆ                    onSignInClick = {
   240ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.SignIn.createRoute())
   241ŌåÆ                    },
   242ŌåÆ                    isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
   243ŌåÆ                )
   244ŌåÆ            }
   245ŌåÆ
   246ŌåÆ            composable(Screen.Search.route) {
   247ŌåÆ                SearchScreen(
   248ŌåÆ                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   249ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   250ŌåÆ                    },
   251ŌåÆ                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   252ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   253ŌåÆ                    },
   254ŌåÆ                    onReplyClick = { postId ->
   255ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(replyTo = postId))
   256ŌåÆ                    },
   257ŌåÆ                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   258ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   259ŌåÆ                    }
   260ŌåÆ                )
   261ŌåÆ            }
   262ŌåÆ
   263ŌåÆ            composable(Screen.Settings.route) {
   264ŌåÆ                SettingsScreen(
   265ŌåÆ                    onSignInClick = {
   266ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.SignIn.createRoute())
   267ŌåÆ                    },
   268ŌåÆ                    onSignOutComplete = {
   269ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(Screen.Explore.route) {
   270ŌåÆ                            popUpTo(0) { inclusive = true }
   271ŌåÆ                        }
   272ŌåÆ                    },
   273ŌåÆ                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   274ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   275ŌåÆ                    },
   276ŌåÆ                    isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
   277ŌåÆ                )
   278ŌåÆ            }
   279ŌåÆ
   280ŌåÆ            composable(
   281ŌåÆ                route = DetailScreen.SignIn.route,
   282ŌåÆ                arguments = listOf(
   283ŌåÆ                    navArgument("token") {
   284ŌåÆ                        type = NavType.StringType
   285ŌåÆ                        nullable = true
   286ŌåÆ                        defaultValue = null
   287ŌåÆ                    },
   288ŌåÆ                    navArgument("code") {
   289ŌåÆ                        type = NavType.StringType
   290ŌåÆ                        nullable = true
   291ŌåÆ                        defaultValue = null
   292ŌåÆ                    }
   293ŌåÆ                )
   294ŌåÆ            ) { backStackEntry ->
   295ŌåÆ                val token = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("token")
   296ŌåÆ                val code = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("code")
   297ŌåÆ                SignInScreen(
   298ŌåÆ                    deepLinkToken = token,
   299ŌåÆ                    deepLinkCode = code,
   300ŌåÆ                    onSignInSuccess = {
   301ŌåÆ                        navController.navigate(Screen.Timeline.route) {
   302ŌåÆ                            popUpTo(0) { inclusive = true }

   310ŌåÆ
   311ŌåÆ            composable(

   339ŌåÆ

   359ŌåÆ                    onPostClick = { id ->

   362ŌåÆ                    isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
   363ŌåÆ                )
   364ŌåÆ            }
   365ŌåÆ
   366ŌåÆ            composable(
   367ŌåÆ                route = DetailScreen.Profile.route,
   368ŌåÆ                arguments = listOf(navArgument("handle") { type = NavType.StringType })
   369ŌåÆ            ) { backStackEntry ->

   371ŌåÆ                ProfileScreen(

   387ŌåÆ                    }
   388ŌåÆ                )
   389ŌåÆ            }
   390ŌåÆ        }

   392ŌåÆ    } // CompositionLocalProvider

    69ŌåÆfun NotificationsScreen(

    87ŌåÆ    }
    88ŌåÆ

    95ŌåÆ
    96ŌåÆ    LaunchedEffect(shouldLoadMore) {

    98ŌåÆ            viewModel.loadMore()

   100ŌåÆ    }
   101ŌåÆ
   102ŌåÆ    Scaffold(

   120ŌåÆ        ) {
   121ŌåÆ            when {

   128ŌåÆ                        onRetry = { viewModel.refresh() }
   129ŌåÆ                    )
   130ŌåÆ                }
   131ŌåÆ                uiState.notifications.isEmpty() -> {
   132ŌåÆ                    ErrorMessage(

   134ŌåÆ                        icon = Icons.Outlined.NotificationsOff

   141ŌåÆ                    ) {
   142ŌåÆ                        LazyColumn(state = listState) {
   143ŌåÆ                            items(

   145ŌåÆ                                key = { it.id }

   149ŌåÆ                                    onPostClick = onPostClick,
   150ŌåÆ                                    onProfileClick = onProfileClick
   151ŌåÆ                                )
   152ŌåÆ                                HorizontalDivider(thickness = 0.5.dp)
   153ŌåÆ                            }
   154ŌåÆ
   155ŌåÆ                            if (uiState.isLoadingMore) {

   159ŌåÆ                            }
   160ŌåÆ                        }
   161ŌåÆ                    }
   162ŌåÆ                }
   163ŌåÆ            }
   164ŌåÆ        }
   165ŌåÆ    }

   168ŌåÆ@Composable
   169ŌåÆprivate fun NotificationItem(
   170ŌåÆ    notification: Notification,
   171ŌåÆ    onPostClick: (String) -> Unit,
   172ŌåÆ    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit
   173ŌåÆ) {
   174ŌåÆ    val (icon, iconColor, actionText) = when (notification) {
   175ŌåÆ        is Notification.Follow -> Triple(
   176ŌåÆ            Icons.Default.PersonAdd,
   177ŌåÆ            Color(0xFF4CAF50),
   178ŌåÆ            stringResource(R.string.notification_follow)
   179ŌåÆ        )
   180ŌåÆ        is Notification.Mention -> Triple(
   181ŌåÆ            Icons.Outlined.AlternateEmail,
   182ŌåÆ            Color(0xFF2196F3),
   183ŌåÆ            stringResource(R.string.notification_mention)
   184ŌåÆ        )
   185ŌåÆ        is Notification.Reply -> Triple(
   186ŌåÆ            Icons.Default.Reply,
   187ŌåÆ            Color(0xFF9C27B0),
   188ŌåÆ            stringResource(R.string.notification_reply)
   189ŌåÆ        )
   190ŌåÆ        is Notification.Quote -> Triple(
   191ŌåÆ            Icons.Default.FormatQuote,
   192ŌåÆ            Color(0xFFFF9800),
   193ŌåÆ            stringResource(R.string.notification_quote)
   194ŌåÆ        )
   195ŌåÆ        is Notification.Share -> Triple(
   196ŌåÆ            Icons.Default.Repeat,
   197ŌåÆ            Color(0xFF00BCD4),
   198ŌåÆ            stringResource(R.string.notification_share)
   199ŌåÆ        )
   200ŌåÆ        is Notification.React -> Triple(
   201ŌåÆ            Icons.Default.Favorite,
   202ŌåÆ            Color(0xFFE91E63),
   203ŌåÆ            notification.emoji?.let { "$it" } ?: stringResource(R.string.notification_react)
   204ŌåÆ        )
   205ŌåÆ    }
   206ŌåÆ
   207ŌåÆ    val post = when (notification) {
   208ŌåÆ        is Notification.Mention -> notification.post
   209ŌåÆ        is Notification.Reply -> notification.post
   210ŌåÆ        is Notification.Quote -> notification.post
   211ŌåÆ        is Notification.Share -> notification.post
   212ŌåÆ        is Notification.React -> notification.post
   213ŌåÆ        else -> null
   214ŌåÆ    }
   215ŌåÆ
   216ŌåÆ    val actor = notification.actors.firstOrNull()
   217ŌåÆ
   218ŌåÆ    val customEmoji = (notification as? Notification.React)?.customEmoji
   219ŌåÆ    val emojiChar = (notification as? Notification.React)?.emoji
   220ŌåÆ
   221ŌåÆ    Row(
   222ŌåÆ        modifier = Modifier
   223ŌåÆ            .fillMaxWidth()
   224ŌåÆ            .clickable {
   225ŌåÆ                when {
   226ŌåÆ                    post != null -> onPostClick(post.id)
   227ŌåÆ                    actor != null -> onProfileClick(actor.handle)
   228ŌåÆ                }
   229ŌåÆ            }
   230ŌåÆ            .padding(12.dp),
   231ŌåÆ        verticalAlignment = Alignment.Top
   232ŌåÆ    ) {
   233ŌåÆ        if (customEmoji != null) {
   234ŌåÆ            AsyncImage(
   235ŌåÆ                model = customEmoji.imageUrl,
   236ŌåÆ                contentDescription = customEmoji.name,
   237ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier.size(24.dp)
   238ŌåÆ            )
   239ŌåÆ        } else if (emojiChar != null) {
   240ŌåÆ            Text(
   241ŌåÆ                text = emojiChar,
   242ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier.size(24.dp),
   243ŌåÆ                style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium
   244ŌåÆ            )
   245ŌåÆ        } else {
   246ŌåÆ            Icon(
   247ŌåÆ                imageVector = icon,
   248ŌåÆ                contentDescription = null,
   249ŌåÆ                tint = iconColor,
   250ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier.size(24.dp)
   251ŌåÆ            )
   252ŌåÆ        }
   253ŌåÆ
   254ŌåÆ        Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(12.dp))
   255ŌåÆ
   256ŌåÆ        Column(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) {
   257ŌåÆ            Row(verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically) {
   258ŌåÆ                if (actor != null) {
   259ŌåÆ                    AsyncImage(
   260ŌåÆ                        model = actor.avatarUrl,
   261ŌåÆ                        contentDescription = null,
   262ŌåÆ                        modifier = Modifier
   263ŌåÆ                            .size(32.dp)
   264ŌåÆ                            .clip(CircleShape)
   265ŌåÆ                            .clickable { onProfileClick(actor.handle) },
   266ŌåÆ                        contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   267ŌåÆ                    )
   268ŌåÆ                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(8.dp))
   269ŌåÆ                }
   270ŌåÆ
   271ŌåÆ                Column(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) {
   272ŌåÆ                    Row(verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically) {
   273ŌåÆ                        val actorDisplayName = actor?.name ?: actor?.handle ?: "Someone"
   274ŌåÆ                        val allActors = notification.actors
   275ŌåÆ                        val actorLabel = when {
   276ŌåÆ                            allActors.size == 2 -> "$actorDisplayName and ${allActors[1].name ?: allActors[1].handle}"
   277ŌåÆ                            allActors.size > 2 -> "$actorDisplayName and ${allActors.size - 1} others"
   278ŌåÆ                            else -> actorDisplayName
   279ŌåÆ                        }
   280ŌåÆ                        Text(
   281ŌåÆ                            text = actorLabel,
   282ŌåÆ                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   283ŌåÆ                            fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   284ŌåÆ                            maxLines = 1,
   285ŌåÆ                            overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   286ŌåÆ                            modifier = Modifier.weight(1f, fill = false)
   287ŌåÆ                        )
   288ŌåÆ                        Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
   289ŌåÆ                        Text(
   290ŌåÆ                            text = actionText,
   291ŌåÆ                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   292ŌåÆ                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   293ŌåÆ                        )
   294ŌåÆ                    }
   295ŌåÆ                    Text(
   296ŌåÆ                        text = formatRelativeTime(notification.created),
   297ŌåÆ                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   298ŌåÆ                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   299ŌåÆ                    )
   300ŌåÆ                }
   301ŌåÆ            }
   302ŌåÆ
   303ŌåÆ            val shouldHavePost = notification is Notification.Mention ||
   304ŌåÆ                notification is Notification.Reply ||
   305ŌåÆ                notification is Notification.Quote ||
   306ŌåÆ                notification is Notification.Share ||
   307ŌåÆ                notification is Notification.React
   308ŌåÆ
   309ŌåÆ            if (post != null) {
   310ŌåÆ                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.size(8.dp))
   311ŌåÆ                HtmlContent(
   312ŌåÆ                    html = post.content,
   313ŌåÆ                    maxLines = 3,
   314ŌåÆ                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   315ŌåÆ                    onMentionClick = onProfileClick
   316ŌåÆ                )
   317ŌåÆ            } else if (shouldHavePost) {
   318ŌåÆ                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.size(8.dp))
   319ŌåÆ                Text(
   320ŌåÆ                    text = stringResource(R.string.notification_post_unavailable),
   321ŌåÆ                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   322ŌåÆ                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant.copy(alpha = 0.6f),
   323ŌåÆ                    fontStyle = androidx.compose.ui.text.font.FontStyle.Italic
   324ŌåÆ                )
   325ŌåÆ            }
   326ŌåÆ        }
   327ŌåÆ    }
   328ŌåÆ}
   329ŌåÆ
   330ŌåÆprivate fun formatRelativeTime(instant: Instant): String {
   331ŌåÆ    val now = Instant.now()
   332ŌåÆ    val duration = Duration.between(instant, now)
   333ŌåÆ
   334ŌåÆ    return when {
   335ŌåÆ        duration.toMinutes() < 1 -> "now"
   336ŌåÆ        duration.toMinutes() < 60 -> "${duration.toMinutes()}m"
   337ŌåÆ        duration.toHours() < 24 -> "${duration.toHours()}h"
   338ŌåÆ        duration.toDays() < 7 -> "${duration.toDays()}d"
   339ŌåÆ        else -> "${duration.toDays() / 7}w"
   340ŌåÆ    }
   341ŌåÆ}

    84ŌåÆ    onPostSuccess: () -> Unit,

    86ŌåÆ    viewModel: ComposeViewModel = hiltViewModel()

    92ŌåÆ
    93ŌåÆ    // Track TextFieldValue for cursor position
    94ŌåÆ    var textFieldValue by remember {
    95ŌåÆ        mutableStateOf(TextFieldValue(text = uiState.content))
    96ŌåÆ    }
    97ŌåÆ
    98ŌåÆ    // Track cursor and text field positions for popup placement
    99ŌåÆ    var cursorRect by remember { mutableStateOf(Rect.Zero) }
   100ŌåÆ    var textFieldBounds by remember { mutableStateOf(Rect.Zero) }
   101ŌåÆ    var textLayoutResult by remember { mutableStateOf<TextLayoutResult?>(null) }
   102ŌåÆ    val scrollState = rememberScrollState()
   103ŌåÆ    val density = LocalDensity.current
   104ŌåÆ    val popupHeight = with(density) { 200.dp.toPx() } // Estimated popup height
   105ŌåÆ
   106ŌåÆ    // Sync TextFieldValue with ViewModel state changes (e.g., when mention is selected)
   107ŌåÆ    LaunchedEffect(uiState.content, uiState.cursorPosition) {
   108ŌåÆ        if (textFieldValue.text != uiState.content) {
   109ŌåÆ            textFieldValue = TextFieldValue(
   110ŌåÆ                text = uiState.content,
   111ŌåÆ                selection = TextRange(uiState.cursorPosition)
   112ŌåÆ            )
   113ŌåÆ        }
   114ŌåÆ    }
   115ŌåÆ
   116ŌåÆ    LaunchedEffect(replyToId) {
   117ŌåÆ        replyToId?.let { viewModel.setReplyTarget(it) }
   118ŌåÆ    }
   119ŌåÆ
   120ŌåÆ    LaunchedEffect(quotedPostId) {

   122ŌåÆ    }

   124ŌåÆ    LaunchedEffect(uiState.isPosted) {

   128ŌåÆ    }

   136ŌåÆ

   139ŌåÆ        topBar = {

   143ŌåÆ                    quotedPostId != null -> stringResource(R.string.quoting)
   144ŌåÆ                    else -> stringResource(R.string.compose)
   145ŌåÆ                },
   146ŌåÆ                navigationIcon = {
   147ŌåÆ                    IconButton(onClick = onNavigateBack) {
   148ŌåÆ                        Icon(
   149ŌåÆ                            imageVector = Icons.Default.Close,
   150ŌåÆ                            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.cancel)
   151ŌåÆ                        )
   152ŌåÆ                    }
   153ŌåÆ                },
   154ŌåÆ                actions = {
   155ŌåÆ                    Button(
   156ŌåÆ                        onClick = { viewModel.post() },
   157ŌåÆ                        enabled = uiState.content.isNotBlank() && !uiState.isPosting
   158ŌåÆ                    ) {
   159ŌåÆ                        Text(stringResource(R.string.post))
   160ŌåÆ                    }
   161ŌåÆ                }
   162ŌåÆ            )

   237ŌåÆ                        }
   238ŌåÆ                    } else {

   242ŌåÆ                        Surface(

   287ŌåÆ                            },
   288ŌåÆ                            onTextLayout = { result: TextLayoutResult ->

   291ŌåÆ                                val cursorPos = textFieldValue.selection.start
   292ŌåÆ                                    .coerceIn(0, textFieldValue.text.length)

   299ŌåÆ                            modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),

   314ŌåÆ                                    innerTextField()

   321ŌåÆ                // Mention autocomplete popup
   322ŌåÆ                if (uiState.mentionSuggestions.isNotEmpty() || uiState.isLoadingMentions) {
   323ŌåÆ                    // Calculate cursor position accounting for padding and scroll
   324ŌåÆ                    val paddingPx = with(density) { 16.dp.toPx() }

   326ŌåÆ                    val cursorXInBox = cursorRect.left + paddingPx
   327ŌåÆ
   328ŌåÆ                    // Clamp to visible area
   329ŌåÆ                    val visibleCursorY = cursorYInBox.coerceIn(paddingPx, textFieldBounds.height - paddingPx)
   330ŌåÆ
   331ŌåÆ                    Popup(

   348ŌåÆ                            },

   350ŌåÆ                        )
   351ŌåÆ                    }
   352ŌåÆ                }
   353ŌåÆ                } // end else (edit mode)
   354ŌåÆ            }
   355ŌåÆ
   356ŌåÆ            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(16.dp))
   357ŌåÆ
   358ŌåÆ            Row(
   359ŌåÆ                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   360ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()
   361ŌåÆ            ) {

   368ŌåÆ                TextButton(

   374ŌåÆ                            PostVisibility.UNLISTED -> Icons.Outlined.Lock

   380ŌåÆ                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
   381ŌåÆ                    Text(

   368ŌåÆ
   369ŌåÆ                DropdownMenu(

   383ŌåÆ                            }
   384ŌåÆ                        )
   385ŌåÆ                    }
   386ŌåÆ                }
   387ŌåÆ            }
   388ŌåÆ        }
   389ŌåÆ    )
   390ŌåÆ}

   378ŌåÆ@Composable
   379ŌåÆprivate fun PostDetailContent(
   380ŌåÆ    post: Post,
   381ŌåÆ    reactionGroups: List<ReactionGroup>,
   382ŌåÆ    replies: List<Post>,
   383ŌåÆ    hasMoreReplies: Boolean = false,
   384ŌåÆ    isLoadingMoreReplies: Boolean = false,
   385ŌåÆ    onLoadMoreReplies: () -> Unit = {},
   386ŌåÆ    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit,
   387ŌåÆ    onPostClick: (String) -> Unit,
   388ŌåÆ    onShareClick: () -> Unit,
   389ŌåÆ    onReplyClick: () -> Unit,

   392ŌåÆ    onQuoteClick: () -> Unit,

   397ŌåÆ    val dateFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("MMM d, yyyy 'at' h:mm a")

   400ŌåÆ    LazyColumn {

   711ŌåÆ@Composable
   712ŌåÆprivate fun SharesSheet(
   713ŌåÆ    actors: List<pub.hackers.android.domain.model.Actor>,
   714ŌåÆ    isLoading: Boolean,
   715ŌåÆ    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit,
   716ŌåÆ    onClose: () -> Unit
   717ŌåÆ) {
   718ŌåÆ    Column(
   719ŌåÆ        modifier = Modifier
   720ŌåÆ            .fillMaxWidth()
   721ŌåÆ            .padding(16.dp)
   722ŌåÆ    ) {
   723ŌåÆ        Text(
   724ŌåÆ            text = stringResource(R.string.shares),
   725ŌåÆ            style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   726ŌåÆ            fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   727ŌåÆ            modifier = Modifier.padding(bottom = 16.dp)
   728ŌåÆ        )
   729ŌåÆ
   730ŌåÆ        if (isLoading) {
   731ŌåÆ            Box(
   732ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier
   733ŌåÆ                    .fillMaxWidth()
   734ŌåÆ                    .height(100.dp),
   735ŌåÆ                contentAlignment = Alignment.Center
   736ŌåÆ            ) {
   737ŌåÆ                CircularProgressIndicator()
   738ŌåÆ            }
   739ŌåÆ        } else if (actors.isEmpty()) {
   740ŌåÆ            Text(
   741ŌåÆ                text = "No shares yet",
   742ŌåÆ                style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   743ŌåÆ                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   744ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier.padding(vertical = 24.dp)
   745ŌåÆ            )
   746ŌåÆ        } else {
   747ŌåÆ            actors.forEach { actor ->
   748ŌåÆ                Row(
   749ŌåÆ                    verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   750ŌåÆ                    modifier = Modifier
   751ŌåÆ                        .fillMaxWidth()
   752ŌåÆ                        .clickable { onProfileClick(actor.handle) }
   753ŌåÆ                        .padding(vertical = 8.dp)
   754ŌåÆ                ) {
   755ŌåÆ                    AsyncImage(
   756ŌåÆ                        model = actor.avatarUrl,

   759ŌåÆ                            .size(40.dp)
   760ŌåÆ                            .clip(CircleShape),
   761ŌåÆ                        contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   762ŌåÆ                    )
   763ŌåÆ                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(12.dp))
   764ŌåÆ                    Column {
   765ŌåÆ                        Text(
   766ŌåÆ                            text = actor.name ?: actor.handle,
   767ŌåÆ                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   768ŌåÆ                            fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold
   769ŌåÆ                        )
   770ŌåÆ                        Text(
   771ŌåÆ                            text = "@${actor.handle}",
   772ŌåÆ                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   773ŌåÆ                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   774ŌåÆ                        )
   775ŌåÆ                    }
   776ŌåÆ                }
   777ŌåÆ            }
   778ŌåÆ        }
   779ŌåÆ    }
   780ŌåÆ}

   861ŌåÆ@Composable
   862ŌåÆprivate fun ReplyTargetPreview(
   863ŌåÆ    post: Post,
   864ŌåÆ    onClick: () -> Unit,
   865ŌåÆ    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit
   866ŌåÆ) {
   867ŌåÆ    Column(
   868ŌåÆ        modifier = Modifier
   869ŌåÆ            .fillMaxWidth()
   870ŌåÆ            .clickable(onClick = onClick)
   871ŌåÆ            .alpha(0.6f)
   872ŌåÆ    ) {
   873ŌåÆ        Row(
   874ŌåÆ            verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   875ŌåÆ        ) {
   876ŌåÆ            AsyncImage(
   877ŌåÆ                model = post.actor.avatarUrl,
   878ŌåÆ                contentDescription = null,
   879ŌåÆ                modifier = Modifier
   880ŌåÆ                    .size(40.dp)
   881ŌåÆ                    .clip(CircleShape)
   882ŌåÆ                    .clickable { onProfileClick(post.actor.handle) },
   883ŌåÆ                contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   884ŌåÆ            )
   885ŌåÆ
   886ŌåÆ            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(12.dp))

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of both the spec and the existing codebase. Let me compile the full review.

---

# Design Specification Review: Hackers'Pub Android UI Redesign

**Spec:** `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-19-android-redesign-design.md`

**Reviewer's summary:** The spec is well-structured and provides a solid foundation for the redesign. It covers the primary design tokens, screen layouts, and migration strategy. However, there are several gaps in completeness, ambiguous dimensions, missing screen coverage, and inconsistencies between the spec's design system and the spec's own screen descriptions that would cause developer confusion during implementation.

---

## BLOCKERS (Must fix before implementation)

### B1. Post Detail Screen is completely absent from the spec

The codebase contains a full `PostDetailScreen` at `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/screens/postdetail/PostDetailScreen.kt` which is one of the most complex screens in the app. It includes reply target previews, full engagement stats, reaction groups with a reaction picker bottom sheet, shares/quotes bottom sheets, post deletion, visibility indicators, and a reply FAB. The spec does not mention this screen at all. A developer would have no guidance on how to restyle it, which is especially problematic because PostDetail's layout differs significantly from PostCard (expanded view with timestamp, full stats row, action bar).

### B2. Bottom navigation bar is not specified

The spec describes individual screens but never specifies the bottom navigation bar itself. The existing codebase in `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/HackersPubApp.kt` has five tabs (Timeline, Notifications, Explore, Search, Settings) using M3 `NavigationBar`. The spec mentions tab labels at 10sp/600 and icon sizing at 22dp, but there is no dedicated section describing the bottom bar's overall height, background color, selected/unselected icon colors, divider line above the bar, or how the tab labels relate to the icons. The icons section says "Active state: filled icon with accent color" and "Inactive state: outlined icon with secondary color", but this guidance is scattered and needs to be consolidated into a bottom bar specification.

### B3. No specification for the `textBody` vs `textPrimary` color for author names in PostCard

The spec says author names in the post card should be "15sp/600" but never assigns a color. The typography table defines `bodyLarge` as 15sp/400, not 15sp/600. The author name weight (600 = SemiBold) does not map to any defined typography style. This is a contradiction: should the developer create a new style, or should they use `bodyLarge` with a manual weight override? The spec needs to either define a `bodyLargeSemiBold` variant or clarify that weight overrides are expected inline.

### B4. Engagement bar semantic colors are incomplete

The spec defines `error`/`reaction` as `#E8453C` for "Heart reactions" and `share` as `#34D399` for "Share/repost active state". However, the existing codebase's engagement bar has five actions: reply, share, reaction (heart), quote, and external share. The spec says engagement icons are "Muted by default, semantic colors when active (red heart filled, green repost)" but does not define:
- What color the reply icon uses when active
- What color the quote icon uses when active
- Whether the external share button changes state at all

This ambiguity would force the developer to make design decisions that should be in the spec.

---

## WARNINGS (Should fix but not blocking)

### W1. Missing `xxl` spacing token usage

The existing `Spacing.kt` defines `xxl = 32.dp` which is used in the codebase (e.g., `32.dp` horizontal margin on sign-in screen). The spec's spacing table only goes up to `xl = 24dp`. The sign-in spec itself says "32dp horizontal margin" and "32dp below subtitle", confirming the need for this value, but the design system does not include it.

### W2. Avatar size discrepancy between spec and codebase

The spec defines timeline avatars as 42dp, but the existing PostCard uses 48dp (`Modifier.size(48.dp)`). The spec defines profile avatars as 80dp, but the existing ProfileHeader uses 96dp. These are intentional design changes, but worth calling out as the spec does not mention this as a deliberate reduction, which may cause confusion about whether it is a mistake or intentional.

### W3. Quoted post spec conflicts with codebase quoted post avatar size

The spec says quoted posts should have a "Smaller avatar (32dp)" but the existing `QuotedPostPreview` uses 20dp. The spec's 32dp would be larger than what exists. This should be confirmed as intentional.

### W4. Profile screen is missing follower/following/post counts specification details

The spec says "Stats row: count (16sp/700) above label (12sp/secondary)" but the existing `ProfileResult` model in `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/domain/model/Models.kt` does not contain follower, following, or post counts. The `Actor` model has no stats fields. Either the data layer needs to be extended (violating the "No changes to ViewModels, repositories, data layer" promise in the Migration Strategy), or the spec needs to acknowledge this is an existing limitation and the stats row should be omitted or noted as a future addition.

### W5. Compose screen title spec is inconsistent with codebase

The spec says the compose title should be "New Post" or "Reply" centered at 17sp/600. However: (a) 17sp is not in the typography table at all, (b) the existing codebase uses three possible titles: "Compose", "Reply", and "Quoting". The spec omits the quoting state entirely.

### W6. No specification for media grid appearance in the new design

The codebase has a full `MediaGrid` composable with 1-image, 2-image, and 3+ image layouts using `RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)`. The spec mentions no media-related styling: no image corner radius, no media aspect ratios, no media container behavior. Since the spec says "No card elevation or shadows anywhere", it should clarify whether media images retain their current rounded corners or adopt a different treatment.

### W7. Notification colors in codebase are hardcoded, not in spec

The existing `NotificationsScreen` uses distinct hardcoded colors for each notification type: green for follow (`#4CAF50`), blue for mention (`#2196F3`), purple for reply (`#9C27B0`), orange for quote (`#FF9800`), cyan for share (`#00BCD4`), pink for react (`#E91E63`). The spec's design philosophy says "near-monochrome palette" with "semantic colors reserved for interactive state changes", but does not specify what colors notification type icons should use. Should they all become `textSecondary`? Should they retain distinct colors? This needs explicit guidance.

### W8. Missing specification for Article type differentiation

The codebase has special handling for `typename == "Article"` in both PostCard (shows an "Article" badge) and PostDetailScreen (uses `headlineLarge` for the title). The spec does not mention article-specific styling at all.

### W9. Font size scaling feature is not addressed

The Settings screen and `HackersPubApp` implement a font size scaling feature (0.75x-2.0x via `LocalFontScale`). The spec's Settings section mentions "Font size slider (0.75x-2.0x)" but the design system's typography section does not address how the custom text styles interact with this scaling mechanism. The spec should clarify whether the custom typography definitions serve as base values that get scaled.

### W10. Repost indicator layout description is ambiguous

The spec says the repost indicator should be "indented to align with post content (48dp left padding)". But the timeline avatar is defined as 42dp with a 12dp gap. That means post content starts at 42+12 = 54dp from the left edge. The 48dp indent would not align with the content. This arithmetic should be reconciled.

---

## NOTES (Nice to have improvements)

### N1. Missing dark theme contrast ratios / accessibility verification

The spec provides exact hex values but does not mention WCAG contrast ratio compliance. For example, `textSecondary` in dark theme is `#737373` on `background` `#171717`. This is a 4.48:1 ratio, which passes AA for normal text but fails AAA. The `textBody` in light theme (`#3A3A3C` on `#FFFFFF`) is 9.73:1, which is excellent. A note confirming accessibility compliance would strengthen the spec.

### N2. No specification for transition or animation between states

The spec's "Out of Scope" section says "Custom animations/transitions (can be added later)". This is fine, but it would help to note whether the current M3 default transitions (e.g., pull-to-refresh animation, bottom sheet entrance) should be preserved or if the developer should use `AnimationSpec.None` to strip them.

### N3. No mention of shimmer/skeleton loading states

The codebase uses a simple `CircularProgressIndicator` for all loading states. If the redesign should introduce shimmer placeholders (common in Ivory-style apps), this should be noted. If not, explicitly stating "Continue using spinner-based loading" prevents scope creep.

### N4. Missing spec for the ReactionPicker bottom sheet

The existing codebase has a `ReactionPicker` component shown in a `ModalBottomSheet` from the PostDetailScreen. This is a non-trivial UI element with emoji selection and loading state. No styling guidance is provided.

### N5. Search bar spec says 12dp border-radius but existing Spacing/Radius system has `xl = 12.dp`

The spec should reference its own design tokens by name rather than raw values to maintain consistency. For example, "Search bar border-radius: `Radius.xl` (12dp)" instead of just "12dp border-radius".

### N6. Settings "App Icons" section is specified but not in codebase

The spec lists "App Icons: Alternative icon selection" as a settings section, but the current SettingsScreen has no such feature. This should be marked as "new feature" or removed from the redesign spec since the document claims "No changes to ViewModels, repositories, data layer."

### N7. The `Radius` object in Spacing.kt is not aligned with spec shapes

The existing `Radius` object defines `sm=4`, `md=6`, `lg=8`, `xl=12`. The spec's shapes table defines pill buttons at 20dp, icon buttons as circles, quoted posts at 8dp. The spec should provide an updated Radius token table, or at minimum reference which Radius tokens map to which UI elements.

### N8. Divider horizontal margin inconsistency

The spec says dividers have "16dp horizontal margin" but the existing codebase uses full-bleed `HorizontalDivider()` calls with no horizontal padding. The spec should clarify whether this is a deliberate change requiring `Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp)` on every divider.

### N9. Icon library migration not fully specified

The spec says "Feather/Lucide style" icons and lists a new file `Icons.kt` in the migration strategy. However, it does not provide a mapping from the current Material Icons (used extensively: `Icons.Filled.Repeat`, `Icons.Outlined.ChatBubbleOutline`, `Icons.Filled.Favorite`, etc.) to specific Feather/Lucide icon names. Without this mapping, the developer will need to make subjective icon choices for every icon in the app.

### N10. No guidance on handle display format

The codebase shows handles as `@handle` in most places. The spec mentions "Handle: 14sp/400, `textSecondary`" for the profile screen but does not specify whether the `@` prefix is part of the display format or handled by the composable. This is minor but worth standardizing.

---

## Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKER  | 4     |
| WARNING  | 10    |
| NOTE     | 10    |

**Key actions required before implementation begins:**

1. Add a Post Detail Screen section to the spec (B1)
2. Add a Bottom Navigation Bar section to the spec (B2)
3. Resolve the author name typography conflict at 15sp/600 (B3)
4. Define engagement icon active colors for all five actions (B4)

The spec is strongest in its color palette and spacing definitions, and the migration strategy is pragmatic. The main risk is that a developer implementing this spec would need to make too many unsupported design decisions for uncovered screens and states, particularly PostDetail (the most complex screen in the app) which is entirely absent.

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of both the spec and the existing codebase. Let me compile the full review.

---

# Design Specification Review: Hackers'Pub Android UI Redesign

**Spec:** `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-19-android-redesign-design.md`

**Reviewer's summary:** The spec is well-structured and provides a solid foundation for the redesign. It covers the primary design tokens, screen layouts, and migration strategy. However, there are several gaps in completeness, ambiguous dimensions, missing screen coverage, and inconsistencies between the spec's design system and the spec's own screen descriptions that would cause developer confusion during implementation.

---

## BLOCKERS (Must fix before implementation)

### B1. Post Detail Screen is completely absent from the spec

The codebase contains a full `PostDetailScreen` at `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/screens/postdetail/PostDetailScreen.kt` which is one of the most complex screens in the app. It includes reply target previews, full engagement stats, reaction groups with a reaction picker bottom sheet, shares/quotes bottom sheets, post deletion, visibility indicators, and a reply FAB. The spec does not mention this screen at all. A developer would have no guidance on how to restyle it, which is especially problematic because PostDetail's layout differs significantly from PostCard (expanded view with timestamp, full stats row, action bar).

### B2. Bottom navigation bar is not specified

The spec describes individual screens but never specifies the bottom navigation bar itself. The existing codebase in `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/HackersPubApp.kt` has five tabs (Timeline, Notifications, Explore, Search, Settings) using M3 `NavigationBar`. The spec mentions tab labels at 10sp/600 and icon sizing at 22dp, but there is no dedicated section describing the bottom bar's overall height, background color, selected/unselected icon colors, divider line above the bar, or how the tab labels relate to the icons. The icons section says "Active state: filled icon with accent color" and "Inactive state: outlined icon with secondary color", but this guidance is scattered and needs to be consolidated into a bottom bar specification.

### B3. No specification for the `textBody` vs `textPrimary` color for author names in PostCard

The spec says author names in the post card should be "15sp/600" but never assigns a color. The typography table defines `bodyLarge` as 15sp/400, not 15sp/600. The author name weight (600 = SemiBold) does not map to any defined typography style. This is a contradiction: should the developer create a new style, or should they use `bodyLarge` with a manual weight override? The spec needs to either define a `bodyLargeSemiBold` variant or clarify that weight overrides are expected inline.

### B4. Engagement bar semantic colors are incomplete

The spec defines `error`/`reaction` as `#E8453C` for "Heart reactions" and `share` as `#34D399` for "Share/repost active state". However, the existing codebase's engagement bar has five actions: reply, share, reaction (heart), quote, and external share. The spec says engagement icons are "Muted by default, semantic colors when active (red heart filled, green repost)" but does not define:
- What color the reply icon uses when active
- What color the quote icon uses when active
- Whether the external share button changes state at all

This ambiguity would force the developer to make design decisions that should be in the spec.

---

## WARNINGS (Should fix but not blocking)

### W1. Missing `xxl` spacing token usage

The existing `Spacing.kt` defines `xxl = 32.dp` which is used in the codebase (e.g., `32.dp` horizontal margin on sign-in screen). The spec's spacing table only goes up to `xl = 24dp`. The sign-in spec itself says "32dp horizontal margin" and "32dp below subtitle", confirming the need for this value, but the design system does not include it.

### W2. Avatar size discrepancy between spec and codebase

The spec defines timeline avatars as 42dp, but the existing PostCard uses 48dp (`Modifier.size(48.dp)`). The spec defines profile avatars as 80dp, but the existing ProfileHeader uses 96dp. These are intentional design changes, but worth calling out as the spec does not mention this as a deliberate reduction, which may cause confusion about whether it is a mistake or intentional.

### W3. Quoted post spec conflicts with codebase quoted post avatar size

The spec says quoted posts should have a "Smaller avatar (32dp)" but the existing `QuotedPostPreview` uses 20dp. The spec's 32dp would be larger than what exists. This should be confirmed as intentional.

### W4. Profile screen is missing follower/following/post counts specification details

The spec says "Stats row: count (16sp/700) above label (12sp/secondary)" but the existing `ProfileResult` model in `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/domain/model/Models.kt` does not contain follower, following, or post counts. The `Actor` model has no stats fields. Either the data layer needs to be extended (violating the "No changes to ViewModels, repositories, data layer" promise in the Migration Strategy), or the spec needs to acknowledge this is an existing limitation and the stats row should be omitted or noted as a future addition.

### W5. Compose screen title spec is inconsistent with codebase

The spec says the compose title should be "New Post" or "Reply" centered at 17sp/600. However: (a) 17sp is not in the typography table at all, (b) the existing codebase uses three possible titles: "Compose", "Reply", and "Quoting". The spec omits the quoting state entirely.

### W6. No specification for media grid appearance in the new design

The codebase has a full `MediaGrid` composable with 1-image, 2-image, and 3+ image layouts using `RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)`. The spec mentions no media-related styling: no image corner radius, no media aspect ratios, no media container behavior. Since the spec says "No card elevation or shadows anywhere", it should clarify whether media images retain their current rounded corners or adopt a different treatment.

### W7. Notification colors in codebase are hardcoded, not in spec

The existing `NotificationsScreen` uses distinct hardcoded colors for each notification type: green for follow (`#4CAF50`), blue for mention (`#2196F3`), purple for reply (`#9C27B0`), orange for quote (`#FF9800`), cyan for share (`#00BCD4`), pink for react (`#E91E63`). The spec's design philosophy says "near-monochrome palette" with "semantic colors reserved for interactive state changes", but does not specify what colors notification type icons should use. Should they all become `textSecondary`? Should they retain distinct colors? This needs explicit guidance.

### W8. Missing specification for Article type differentiation

The codebase has special handling for `typename == "Article"` in both PostCard (shows an "Article" badge) and PostDetailScreen (uses `headlineLarge` for the title). The spec does not mention article-specific styling at all.

### W9. Font size scaling feature is not addressed

The Settings screen and `HackersPubApp` implement a font size scaling feature (0.75x-2.0x via `LocalFontScale`). The spec's Settings section mentions "Font size slider (0.75x-2.0x)" but the design system's typography section does not address how the custom text styles interact with this scaling mechanism. The spec should clarify whether the custom typography definitions serve as base values that get scaled.

### W10. Repost indicator layout description is ambiguous

The spec says the repost indicator should be "indented to align with post content (48dp left padding)". But the timeline avatar is defined as 42dp with a 12dp gap. That means post content starts at 42+12 = 54dp from the left edge. The 48dp indent would not align with the content. This arithmetic should be reconciled.

---

## NOTES (Nice to have improvements)

### N1. Missing dark theme contrast ratios / accessibility verification

The spec provides exact hex values but does not mention WCAG contrast ratio compliance. For example, `textSecondary` in dark theme is `#737373` on `background` `#171717`. This is a 4.48:1 ratio, which passes AA for normal text but fails AAA. The `textBody` in light theme (`#3A3A3C` on `#FFFFFF`) is 9.73:1, which is excellent. A note confirming accessibility compliance would strengthen the spec.

### N2. No specification for transition or animation between states

The spec's "Out of Scope" section says "Custom animations/transitions (can be added later)". This is fine, but it would help to note whether the current M3 default transitions (e.g., pull-to-refresh animation, bottom sheet entrance) should be preserved or if the developer should use `AnimationSpec.None` to strip them.

### N3. No mention of shimmer/skeleton loading states

The codebase uses a simple `CircularProgressIndicator` for all loading states. If the redesign should introduce shimmer placeholders (common in Ivory-style apps), this should be noted. If not, explicitly stating "Continue using spinner-based loading" prevents scope creep.

### N4. Missing spec for the ReactionPicker bottom sheet

The existing codebase has a `ReactionPicker` component shown in a `ModalBottomSheet` from the PostDetailScreen. This is a non-trivial UI element with emoji selection and loading state. No styling guidance is provided.

### N5. Search bar spec says 12dp border-radius but existing Spacing/Radius system has `xl = 12.dp`

The spec should reference its own design tokens by name rather than raw values to maintain consistency. For example, "Search bar border-radius: `Radius.xl` (12dp)" instead of just "12dp border-radius".

### N6. Settings "App Icons" section is specified but not in codebase

The spec lists "App Icons: Alternative icon selection" as a settings section, but the current SettingsScreen has no such feature. This should be marked as "new feature" or removed from the redesign spec since the document claims "No changes to ViewModels, repositories, data layer."

### N7. The `Radius` object in Spacing.kt is not aligned with spec shapes

The existing `Radius` object defines `sm=4`, `md=6`, `lg=8`, `xl=12`. The spec's shapes table defines pill buttons at 20dp, icon buttons as circles, quoted posts at 8dp. The spec should provide an updated Radius token table, or at minimum reference which Radius tokens map to which UI elements.

### N8. Divider horizontal margin inconsistency

The spec says dividers have "16dp horizontal margin" but the existing codebase uses full-bleed `HorizontalDivider()` calls with no horizontal padding. The spec should clarify whether this is a deliberate change requiring `Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp)` on every divider.

### N9. Icon library migration not fully specified

The spec says "Feather/Lucide style" icons and lists a new file `Icons.kt` in the migration strategy. However, it does not provide a mapping from the current Material Icons (used extensively: `Icons.Filled.Repeat`, `Icons.Outlined.ChatBubbleOutline`, `Icons.Filled.Favorite`, etc.) to specific Feather/Lucide icon names. Without this mapping, the developer will need to make subjective icon choices for every icon in the app.

### N10. No guidance on handle display format

The codebase shows handles as `@handle` in most places. The spec mentions "Handle: 14sp/400, `textSecondary`" for the profile screen but does not specify whether the `@` prefix is part of the display format or handled by the composable. This is minor but worth standardizing.

---

## Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKER  | 4     |
| WARNING  | 10    |
| NOTE     | 10    |

**Key actions required before implementation begins:**

1. Add a Post Detail Screen section to the spec (B1)
2. Add a Bottom Navigation Bar section to the spec (B2)
3. Resolve the author name typography conflict at 15sp/600 (B3)
4. Define engagement icon active colors for all five actions (B4)

The spec is strongest in its color palette and spacing definitions, and the migration strategy is pragmatic. The main risk is that a developer implementing this spec would need to make too many unsupported design decisions for uncovered screens and states, particularly PostDetail (the most complex screen in the app) which is entirely absent.

# Hackers'Pub Android ŌĆö Full UI Redesign

**Date:** 2026-03-19
**Status:** Approved
**Goal:** Replace the default Material Design 3 styling with a custom, Ivory-inspired design system that gives Hackers'Pub a distinctive, editorial identity across both light and dark themes.

## Design Philosophy

Platform-agnostic custom identity ŌĆö not Material, not iOS-native, but a unique Hackers'Pub look inspired by Ivory (Tapbots' Mastodon client). Near-monochrome palette with Deep Stone accent. Typography-first: font weight carries emphasis rather than color. Semantic colors (red, green) reserved for interactive state changes, giving them maximum impact against the neutral base.

## Design System

### Color Palette

#### Light Theme
| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `background` | `#FFFFFF` | Screen background |
| `surface` | `#F2F2F7` | Dividers, secondary surfaces, icon button backgrounds |
| `textPrimary` | `#1C1C1E` | Headlines, names, primary content |
| `textBody` | `#3A3A3C` | Body text, post content |
| `textSecondary` | `#AEAEB2` | Handles, timestamps, inactive tab labels |
| `accent` | `#44403C` | Active tab icons/labels, links (via weight), follow buttons, interactive elements |
| `accentMuted` | `#78716C` | Hashtags, relationship tags background at 10% opacity |
| `divider` | `#F2F2F7` | Hairline separators between posts |
| `buttonOutline` | `#D6D3D1` | Secondary button borders |
| `error` / `reaction` | `#E8453C` | Heart reactions, error states, notification dots |
| `share` | `#34D399` | Share/repost active state |

#### Dark Theme
| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `background` | `#171717` | Screen background |
| `surface` | `#262626` | Dividers, secondary surfaces, icon button backgrounds |
| `textPrimary` | `#FAFAFA` | Headlines, names |
| `textBody` | `#D4D4D4` | Body text |
| `textSecondary` | `#737373` | Handles, timestamps, inactive tabs |
| `accent` | `#D6D3D1` | Active tab icons/labels, interactive elements (inverted stone) |
| `accentMuted` | `#A8A29E` | Hashtags, tags |
| `divider` | `#262626` | Hairline separators |
| `buttonOutline` | `#525252` | Secondary button borders |
| `error` / `reaction` | `#E8453C` | Same as light |
| `share` | `#34D399` | Same as light |

### Typography

System default (Roboto on Android). No custom fonts ŌĆö emphasis via weight hierarchy only.

| Style | Size | Weight | Usage |
|-------|------|--------|-------|
| `titleLarge` | 22sp | 700 (Bold) | Screen titles (Timeline, Explore, etc.) |
| `titleMedium` | 20sp | 700 (Bold) | Profile name |
| `bodyLarge` | 15sp | 400 (Regular) | Post content, bio text |
| `bodyMedium` | 14sp | 400 (Regular) | Stats labels, settings items |
| `labelMedium` | 13sp | 400 (Regular) | Handles, timestamps, engagement counts |
| `labelSmall` | 12sp | 500 (Medium) | Hashtags, relationship tags |
| `caption` | 11sp | 500 (Medium) | Tag pills, repost indicators |
| `tabLabel` | 10sp | 600 (SemiBold) | Bottom tab labels |

**Letter spacing:** -0.3sp on `titleLarge` for tighter headline feel. Default elsewhere.

### Spacing

Base unit: 4dp. Consistent throughout.

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `xs` | 4dp | Fine spacing (between name and handle) |
| `sm` | 8dp | Compact spacing (engagement icon gaps) |
| `md` | 12dp | Element spacing (avatar-to-text gap, between post sections) |
| `lg` | 16dp | Standard padding (screen edges, post padding) |
| `xl` | 24dp | Section spacing (stats row gaps) |

### Shapes

| Element | Radius | Size |
|---------|--------|------|
| Avatar (timeline) | Circle | 42dp |
| Avatar (profile) | Circle | 80dp |
| Avatar (repost indicator) | Circle | 16dp |
| Pill buttons (Follow, etc.) | 20dp | Height ~36dp |
| Icon buttons (compose, etc.) | Circle | 28dp |
| Relationship tags | Full capsule | Auto-width |
| Notification dot | Circle | 8dp |

No card elevation or shadows anywhere. Post separation via hairline dividers only.

### Icons

Outlined stroke icons (Feather/Lucide style), 22dp for tab bar, 16dp for engagement bar. Stroke width 1.5dp.

- Active state: filled icon with accent color
- Inactive state: outlined icon with secondary color
- Engagement active states: filled with semantic color (red heart, green repost)

## Screen Specifications

### Timeline Screen

**Header:**
- Large bold title "Timeline" (22sp/700) top-left
- Right side: compose button (28dp circle, surface background, + icon) and user avatar (28dp circle)
- No border below header ŌĆö content starts immediately

**Content:**
- Vertical scrolling list (LazyColumn)
- Posts separated by 1dp hairline dividers (`divider` color) with 16dp horizontal margin
- Pull-to-refresh support

**Post Card Layout:**
- 16dp horizontal padding, 12dp vertical padding
- Repost indicator (if shared): 16dp avatar + repost icon + actor name, caption size, secondary color, indented to align with post content (48dp left padding)
- Author row: 42dp circular avatar, 12dp gap, then name (15sp/600) + timestamp (13sp/secondary) on same baseline
- Body text: 15sp/400, `textBody` color, 4dp below author row
- Hashtags: 11sp/500, `accentMuted` color, 6dp below body
- Engagement bar: 10dp below content, icons (16dp) + counts (13sp), 18dp gaps between items. Muted by default, semantic colors when active (red heart filled, green repost)

**Quoted Post (if present):**
- Indented block below body text
- 1dp border in `divider` color, 8dp border-radius, 8dp padding
- Smaller avatar (32dp), author info, truncated content

**Floating Action Button:** None ŌĆö compose is in the header

### Profile Screen

**Header:** Back arrow (left) + ellipsis menu (right), no title text

**Profile Section (centered):**
- 80dp circular avatar
- Name: 20sp/700, 12dp below avatar
- Handle: 14sp/400, `textSecondary`, 2dp below name
- Bio: 14sp/400, `textBody`, centered, 10dp below handle, 24dp horizontal margin
- Stats row: 24dp gap between items, centered, 14dp below bio
  - Each stat: count (16sp/700) above label (12sp/secondary)
  - Three items: Posts, Followers, Following
- Action buttons: 16dp below stats, centered
  - Follow: pill button, `accent` background, white text, 28dp horizontal padding
  - Share: pill button, transparent, `buttonOutline` border, share icon only
- Relationship tag: "Follows you" capsule, `accentMuted` at 10% opacity background, 11sp/500, 10dp below buttons

**Content:** Hairline divider, then post list identical to timeline (without avatars if showing own profile ŌĆö keep avatars for consistency)

### Notifications Screen

**Header:** Large bold title "Notifications" (same as timeline)

**Notification Rows:**
- 42dp actor avatar (circular), 12dp gap
- Action text: actor name (bold) + action description + timestamp
- Notification type indicated by subtle icon or text ("liked your post", "shared your post", "followed you")
- Hairline dividers between items
- Unread indicator: red dot (8dp) on the Alerts tab icon
- Empty state: centered text "No notifications yet"

### Explore Screen

**Header:** Large bold title "Explore"

**Segmented Tabs:** Below header, "Local" / "Global" segmented control
- Active tab: `accent` text, underline indicator
- Inactive tab: `textSecondary`

**Content:** Same post list as timeline, scoped to local or global feed

### Search Screen

**Header:** Large bold title "Search"

**Search Bar:** Rounded text field below header, `surface` background, 12dp border-radius, search icon left-aligned, 16dp horizontal margin

**Results:** Actor rows (avatar + name + handle) and post cards, depending on result type

### Compose Screen

**Header:** Navigation bar style
- Left: "Cancel" text button (`accent` color)
- Right: "Post" pill button (`accent` background, white text), disabled state at 40% opacity
- Title: "New Post" or "Reply" centered (17sp/600)

**Content:**
- Reply indicator (if replying): "Replying to @handle" in `textSecondary`, 13sp
- Text editor: full-width, 15sp, placeholder "What's on your mind?"
- Markdown support with live preview toggle

**Bottom toolbar:** Visibility selector, language picker ŌĆö subtle icons in `textSecondary`

### Settings Screen

**Header:** Large bold title "Settings"

**Content:** Grouped list sections with section headers (13sp/500, uppercase, `textSecondary`)

Sections:
- **App Info:** Logo (80dp, 16dp corner radius), app name, version
- **Typography:** Font size slider (0.75xŌĆō2.0x)
- **Timeline:** Markdown truncation options
- **Engagement:** Confirmation toggles
- **Links:** In-app browser toggle
- **App Icons:** Alternative icon selection

Each row: standard list item, 16dp padding, `textPrimary` label, `textSecondary` value/description. Hairline dividers between rows.

### Sign-In Screen

**Layout:** Centered vertically

- Logo: 120dp, centered
- Title: "Hackers' Pub" (22sp/700), 16dp below logo
- Subtitle: "Sign in to continue" (14sp, `textSecondary`), 4dp below title
- Username field: rounded border (12dp radius), `surface` background, 32dp below subtitle, 32dp horizontal margin
- Verification code field: same style (appears after username submission)
- Primary button: pill shape, `accent` background, white text, full width within margins
- Error text: `error` color, 13sp, below button

## Migration Strategy

This redesign replaces the existing Material Design 3 theming while keeping the same Compose architecture. Changes are purely in the UI layer:

1. **Theme.kt** ŌĆö Replace M3 color scheme with custom color tokens
2. **Spacing.kt** ŌĆö Already exists, minor adjustments to values
3. **New: Typography.kt** ŌĆö Define custom text styles (not M3 Typography)
4. **New: Shapes.kt** ŌĆö Define shape tokens
5. **New: Icons.kt** ŌĆö Custom icon set (if switching from Material Icons to Feather/Lucide)
6. **Each screen** ŌĆö Replace M3 components (TopAppBar, FloatingActionButton, etc.) with custom composables
7. **PostCard** ŌĆö Restyle engagement bar, remove card elevation, update spacing
8. **CompactTopBar** ŌĆö Replace with new LargeTitle header composable

No changes to ViewModels, repositories, data layer, or navigation graph. Pure visual transformation.

## Out of Scope

- Custom animations/transitions (can be added later)
- Custom font families (using system Roboto)
- Tablet/landscape layouts
- Widget design
- Onboarding flow

| `bodyLargeSemiBold` | 15sp | 600 (SemiBold) | Author names in post cards |
| `bodyLarge` | 15sp | 400 (Regular) | Post content, bio text |
